nodes.label:
☁️ Scale padding
Formula: PaddedMonthly = MonthlyAdSpend * 1.2
Daily = PaddedMonthly / 30
Used for: realistic scale budgeting
Example use: avoid underfunding when you raise spend
nodes.label_raw:
☁️ Scale padding
Formula: PaddedMonthly = MonthlyAdSpend * 1.2
Daily = PaddedMonthly / 30
Used for: realistic scale budgeting
Example use: avoid underfunding when you raise spend

nodes.label:
☁️ Reverse budget from goal
Formula: MonthlyAdSpend = TargetCustomers * CAC
Used for: budget planning from bookings goal
Example use: set seasonal promo spend based on required bookings
nodes.label_raw:
☁️ Reverse budget from goal
Formula: MonthlyAdSpend = TargetCustomers * CAC

nodes.label:
☁️ Test budget cap (rule of thumb)
Formula: MaxTestSpend ~ 2x 30-day cash collected
Used for: controlling test risk
Example use: stop overspending before proof of engagement exists
nodes.label_raw:
☁️ Test budget cap (rule of thumb)
Formula: MaxTestSpend ~ 2x 30-day cash collected
Used for: controlling test risk
Example use: stop overspending before proof of engagement exists

nodes.label:
☁️ Affiliate COGS estimate
Formula: COGS = TotalSales * COGS_percent
Used for: margin planning before commissions
Example use: avoid overpaying when fulfillment costs rise
nodes.label_raw:
☁️ Affiliate COGS estimate
Formula: COGS = TotalSales * COGS_percent
Used for: margin planning before commissions
Example use: avoid overpaying when fulfillment costs rise

nodes.label:
☁️ Affiliate gross profit estimate
Formula: GP = TotalSales - COGS
Used for: commission headroom
Example use: set tiered payouts that stay profitable across packages
nodes.label_raw:
☁️ Affiliate gross profit estimate
Formula: GP = TotalSales - COGS

Example use: set tiered payouts that stay profitable across packages

nodes.label:
☁️ Affiliate payout estimate
Formula: Payout = GP * Payout_percent
Used for: designing tiered commissions under CAC limits
Example use: increase payout only when volume/quality stays strong
nodes.label_raw:
☁️ Affiliate payout estimate

nodes.label:
☁️ Affiliate net return check
Formula: NetReturn = GP_from_aff_customers - Cost_to_get_affiliate
Used for: deciding if a partner is worth recruiting
Example use: compare sponsoring partner vs spending same on ads
nodes.label_raw:
☁️ Affiliate net return check

nodes.label:
☁️ Affiliate total sales forecast
Formula: TotalSales = MonthlySales * MonthsActive
Used for: estimating partner long-term value
Example use: prioritize support for highest expected output

☁️ Affiliate total sales forecast

nodes.label:
☁️ CAC from shows
Formula: CAC = CostPerShow / CloseRate
Used for: setting max cost per booked call
Example use: decide if improving sales close rate beats buying more leads
nodes.label_raw:
☁️ CAC from shows
Formula: CAC = CostPerShow / CloseRate
Used for: setting max cost per booked call

nodes.label:
☁️ Client-Financed Acquisition (CFA)
Goal: 30DayGP >= CAC
Formula: 30DayGP = 30DayRevenue - 30DayDeliveryCosts
Used for: scaling without cashflow stress
Example use: ensure deposits/near-term GP covers acquisition
nodes.label_raw:
☁️ Client-Financed Acquisition (CFA)
Goal: 30DayGP >= CAC
Formula: 30DayGP = 30DayRevenue - 30DayDeliveryCosts
Used for: scaling without cashflow stress
Example use: ensure deposits/near-term GP covers acquisition

nodes.label:
☁️ Churn rate
Formula: ChurnRate = Churned / TotalCustomers
Used for: retention health check
Example use: decide if delivery/onboarding is causing drop-off
nodes.label_raw:
☁️ Churn rate
Formula: ChurnRate = Churned / TotalCustomers
Used for: retention health check
Example use: decide if delivery/onboarding is causing drop-off

nodes.label:
☁️ Conversion rate (any step)
Formula: CR = Outcomes / Inputs
Used for: finding bottlenecks
Example use: identify if drop is from hook, page, or follow-up

☁️ Conversion rate (any step)

nodes.label:
☁️ Cost per show (appointment)
Formula: CostPerShow = TotalCost / Shows
Used for: pricing the setter engine
Example use: compare in-house setter vs agency cost per show

☁️ Cost per show (appointment)

nodes.label:
☁️ Cost per engaged lead (employees)
Formula: CPEL = TotalPayroll / TotalEngagedLeads
Used for: efficiency + hiring decisions
Example use: decide to hire another setter vs improve training/tools

nodes.label:
☁️ DMs -> engaged leads
Formula: EL = DMs * ReplyRate
Used for: daily DM targets + script testing
Example use: compare DM scripts by reply rate

nodes.label:
☁️ Engaged leads per customer
Formula: EL_per_customer = 1 / Engaged_to_Customer_CR
Used for: translating leads into customers
Example use: check if lead volume supports the revenue target

☁️ Engaged leads/day
Formula: EL_day = count of engaged leads today
Used for: daily output tracking
Example use: compare which campaign produced more engaged leads

Formula: EL_hr = EngagedLeads / HoursWorked

Example use: check whether a new script/tool increased throughput

nodes.label:
☁️ Cold email -> engaged leads
Formula: EL = Sends * OpenRate * ReplyRate
Used for: choosing what to test
Example use: low opens -> test subject; low replies -> test offer/CTA

nodes.label:
☁️ CAC from employees
Formula: CAC = CPEL * EL_per_customer
Used for: compare hiring vs ads
Example use: pick the cheaper/cleaner growth path

Formula: GP = Price - DeliveryCost
Used for: safe CAC/reward limits

☁️ LTGP (Lifetime Gross Profit)
Formula: LTGP = LifetimeRevenue - LifetimeDeliveryCosts
Used for: deciding max allowable CAC
Example use: justify higher CAC for repeat clients or retainers

☁️ LTGP:CAC efficiency
Formula: LTGP_to_CAC = LTGP / CAC
Used for: scale readiness (scale vs fix)
Example use: decide to scale ads or improve retention/onboarding first

nodes.label:
☁️ Referral growth equation
Formula: NetReferralGrowth = Referrals(in) - Churn(out)
Used for: knowing if referrals compound growth
Example use: if net is flat, fix churn or referral ask timing

nodes.label:
☁️ Referral payout anchor (one-sided)
Rule: IncentiveMax ~ allowable CAC
Used for: safe reward sizing
Example use: choose cash vs credit without breaking margins

nodes.label:
☁️ Referral payout anchor (two-sided)
Rule: each side ~ allowable CAC / 2
Used for: structuring friend + referrer incentives
Example use: reward both sides while keeping total under CAC

nodes.label:
☁️ CAC sanity check vs market
Rule: if CAC <= 3x market avg -> improve LTGP
if CAC > 3x market avg -> fix CAC
Used for: pick the right lever first
Example use: decide if targeting/offer is the real issue

5 Go back to #1 (often next = Employees)

nodes.label:
CALC
30-Day Profits ≫ Customer Acquisition Cost + Delivery Cost

Used for: validate Upsell profitability inside the 30-day window.
Example use case: choosing the next-step add-on that pushes profit above acquisition + fulfillment.
nodes.label_raw:
CALC
30-Day Profits ≫ Customer Acquisition Cost + Delivery Cost

Used for: validate Upsell profitability inside the 30-day window.
Example use case: choosing the next-step add-on that pushes profit above acquisition + fulfillment.

nodes.label:
CALC
Cash Collected Up Front ≥ Customer Acquisition Cost + Delivery Cost

Used for: ensure Attraction Offer “covers our costs.”
Example use case: setting an intro package price so ads + delivery are covered.
nodes.label_raw:
CALC
Cash Collected Up Front ≥ Customer Acquisition Cost + Delivery Cost

Used for: ensure Attraction Offer “covers our costs.”
Example use case: setting an intro package price so ads + delivery are covered.

nodes.label:
CALC
Bonus-Only Offer Price = (First Month of Continuity+Bonus Offer Price) × Bonus-Only Offer Multiplier
Bonus-Only Offer Multiplier ∈ [1.33, 2.66]

Used for: separate “bonuses only” (up-front cash) from continuity+bonuses (recurring).
Example use case: selling bonuses standalone to raise cash while still stacking recurring revenue.
nodes.label_raw:
CALC
Bonus-Only Offer Price = (First Month of Continuity+Bonus Offer Price) × Bonus-Only Offer Multiplier
Bonus-Only Offer Multiplier ∈ [1.33, 2.66]

Used for: separate “bonuses only” (up-front cash) from continuity+bonuses (recurring).
Example use case: selling bonuses standalone to raise cash while still stacking recurring revenue.

nodes.label:
CALC
Cancellation Fee = Discount They Agreed To Get (With Their Commitment)

Used for: set cancellation terms that return customer to the month-to-month rate logic.
Example use case: charging back the saved discount if they break commitment early.
nodes.label_raw:
CALC
Cancellation Fee = Discount They Agreed To Get (With Their Commitment)

Used for: set cancellation terms that return customer to the month-to-month rate logic.
Example use case: charging back the saved discount if they break commitment early.

nodes.label:
CALC
Total Term Length = Paid Term Length + Free Term Length
(Extend the term, don’t eat into the term.)

Used for: design continuity discounts as extra time, not reduced paid months.
Example use case: adding free time *after* the paid term ends.
nodes.label_raw:
CALC
Total Term Length = Paid Term Length + Free Term Length
(Extend the term, don’t eat into the term.)

Used for: design continuity discounts as extra time, not reduced paid months.
Example use case: adding free time *after* the paid term ends.

nodes.label:
CALC
Pay Now Options Discount Percentage ∈ [20%, 50%]

Used for: structure the Pay Less Now / Pay More Later follow-up offer.
Example use case: offering a “pay-now” upgrade with a hefty discount + better bonuses.
nodes.label_raw:
CALC
Pay Now Options Discount Percentage ∈ [20%, 50%]

Used for: structure the Pay Less Now / Pay More Later follow-up offer.
Example use case: offering a “pay-now” upgrade with a hefty discount + better bonuses.

nodes.label:
CALC
Total Charged = Base Price + (Base Price × 3% Processing Fee)

Used for: add processing fee to raise topline without changing delivery.
Example use case: charging a processing fee on card-based payments.
nodes.label_raw:
CALC
Total Charged = Base Price + (Base Price × 3% Processing Fee)

Used for: add processing fee to raise topline without changing delivery.
Example use case: charging a processing fee on card-based payments.

nodes.label:
CALC
Rollover Upsell Credit = (Some or All of Previous Purchases) Applied Toward Next Offer

Used for: convert prior spend into commitment toward the next offer (credit mechanic).
Example use case: applying past payments as a credit toward a longer agreement / higher tier.
nodes.label_raw:
CALC
Rollover Upsell Credit = (Some or All of Previous Purchases) Applied Toward Next Offer

Used for: convert prior spend into commitment toward the next offer (credit mechanic).
Example use case: applying past payments as a credit toward a longer agreement / higher tier.

nodes.label:
CALC
Standalone Offer Price = Continuity Monthly Price × Standalone Offer Multiplier
Standalone Offer Multiplier ∈ {1.33, 1.66, 2.00, 2.33, 2.66}

Used for: control what share chooses continuity vs standalone.
Example use case: setting a one-time option high enough that more buyers choose membership.
nodes.label_raw:
CALC
Standalone Offer Price = Continuity Monthly Price × Standalone Offer Multiplier
Standalone Offer Multiplier ∈ {1.33, 1.66, 2.00, 2.33, 2.66}

Used for: control what share chooses continuity vs standalone.
Example use case: setting a one-time option high enough that more buyers choose membership.

nodes.label:
CALC
Startup Fee (Typical) = Monthly Rate × (3 to 5)
Startup Fee (If you want more up-front cash) = Monthly Rate × (1.5 to 3)

Used for: Waived Fee Offer mechanics (month-to-month pays a big fee; commitment waives it).
Example use case: offering “month-to-month with fee” vs “annual commitment with fee waived.”
nodes.label_raw:
CALC
Startup Fee (Typical) = Monthly Rate × (3 to 5)
Startup Fee (If you want more up-front cash) = Monthly Rate × (1.5 to 3)

Used for: Waived Fee Offer mechanics (month-to-month pays a big fee; commitment waives it).
Example use case: offering “month-to-month with fee” vs “annual commitment with fee waived.”

nodes.label:
CALC
Annual Cycles (Every Four Weeks) = 13
Annual Months (Monthly) = 12
Difference = 8.3% More Annually (Every Four Weeks vs Monthly)

Used for: choose billing cadence that increases annual take without changing work.
Example use case: billing every four weeks instead of monthly for the same offer.
